revision.comment: Fixed the image in the infobox. Please see [[Category:Pages using infoboxes with thumbnail images]].
revision.text:
{{Short description|Samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156 (1139–1170)}}{{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Tametomo | image = File:Minamoto-no-Tametomo-by-Kuniyoshi-Utagawa.png | death_date = {{death date and age|1170|4|23|1139|df=y}} | death_place = [[Higo Province]], Japan | native_name = 源 為朝 | native_name_lang = Japanese | birth_date = 1139 A.D. | parents = [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]] (father) Unknown (mother) | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | battles = [[Hōgen Rebellion]], [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]] }} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Tametomo'''|源 為朝||1139 – April 23, 1170}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''[[Chinzei]] Hachirō Tametomo'''|鎮西 八郎 為朝|}}, was a samurai who fought in the [[Hōgen Rebellion]] of 1156. He was the son of [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]], and brother to [[Minamoto no Yukiie|Yukiie]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo|Yoshitomo]].
Tametomo is known in the epic chronicles as a powerful archer and it is said that he once sunk an entire [[Taira]] ship with a single arrow by puncturing its hull below the waterline. It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]], along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo]], his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of [[Izu Ōshima|Ōshima]] in the [[Izu Islands]]. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing [[seppuku]]. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''[[seppuku]]'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the [[1754 Hōreki River incident]].<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老町の歴史文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away [[Smallpox demon|smallpox demons]], in an 1890 print by [[Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Yoshitoshi]] File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>
==See Also== *[[Hokusai]] *[[Takizawa Bakin]] *[[Shunten]] *[[King of Ryukyu]]
==References== {{commons category|Minamoto no Tametomo}} {{reflist|30em}}

He was the son of [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]], and brother to [[Minamoto no Yukiie|Yukiie]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo|Yoshitomo]].

[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away [[Smallpox demon|smallpox demons]], in an 1890 print by [[Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Yoshitoshi]] File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>

{{Short description|Samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156 (1139–1170)}}{{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Tametomo | image = File:Minamoto-no-Tametomo-by-Kuniyoshi-Utagawa.png | death_date = {{death date and age|1170|4|23|1139|df=y}} | death_place = [[Higo Province]], Japan | native_name = 源 為朝 | native_name_lang = Japanese | birth_date = 1139 A.D. | parents = [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]] (father) Unknown (mother) | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | battles = [[Hōgen Rebellion]], [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]] }} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Tametomo'''|源 為朝||1139 – April 23, 1170}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''[[Chinzei]] Hachirō Tametomo'''|鎮西 八郎 為朝|}}, was a samurai who fought in the [[Hōgen Rebellion]] of 1156. He was the son of [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]], and brother to [[Minamoto no Yukiie|Yukiie]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo|Yoshitomo]].
Tametomo is known in the epic chronicles as a powerful archer and it is said that he once sunk an entire [[Taira]] ship with a single arrow by puncturing its hull below the waterline. It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]], along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo]], his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of [[Izu Ōshima|Ōshima]] in the [[Izu Islands]]. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing [[seppuku]]. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''[[seppuku]]'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the [[1754 Hōreki River incident]].<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老町の歴史文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away [[Smallpox demon|smallpox demons]], in an 1890 print by [[Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Yoshitoshi]] File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>

{{Short description|Samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156 (1139–1170)}}{{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Tametomo | image = File:Minamoto-no-Tametomo-by-Kuniyoshi-Utagawa.png | death_date = {{death date and age|1170|4|23|1139|df=y}} | death_place = [[Higo Province]], Japan | native_name = 源 為朝 | native_name_lang = Japanese | birth_date = 1139 A.D. | parents = [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]] (father) Unknown (mother) | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | battles = [[Hōgen Rebellion]], [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]] }} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Tametomo'''|源 為朝||1139 – April 23, 1170}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''[[Chinzei]] Hachirō Tametomo'''|鎮西 八郎 為朝|}}, was a samurai who fought in the [[Hōgen Rebellion]] of 1156. He was the son of [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]], and brother to [[Minamoto no Yukiie|Yukiie]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo|Yoshitomo]].
Tametomo is known in the epic chronicles as a powerful archer and it is said that he once sunk an entire [[Taira]] ship with a single arrow by puncturing its hull below the waterline. It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]], along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo]], his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of [[Izu Ōshima|Ōshima]] in the [[Izu Islands]]. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing [[seppuku]]. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''[[seppuku]]'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the [[1754 Hōreki River incident]].<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老町の歴史文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away [[Smallpox demon|smallpox demons]], in an 1890 print by [[Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Yoshitoshi]] File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>
==See Also== *[[Hokusai]] *[[Takizawa Bakin]] *[[Shunten]] *[[King of Ryukyu]]
==References== {{commons category|Minamoto no Tametomo}} {{reflist|30em}}

He was the son of [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]], and brother to [[Minamoto no Yukiie|Yukiie]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo|Yoshitomo]].

The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of [[Izu Ōshima|Ōshima]] in the [[Izu Islands]]. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing [[seppuku]]. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''[[seppuku]]'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the [[1754 Hōreki River incident]].<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老町の歴史文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away [[Smallpox demon|smallpox demons]], in an 1890 print by [[Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Yoshitoshi]] File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>

It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]], along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo]], his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of [[Izu Ōshima|Ōshima]] in the [[Izu Islands]]. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing [[seppuku]]. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''[[seppuku]]'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the [[1754 Hōreki River incident]].<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老町の歴史文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away [[Smallpox demon|smallpox demons]], in an 1890 print by [[Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Yoshitoshi]] File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>

{{Short description|Samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156 (1139–1170)}}{{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Tametomo | image = File:Minamoto-no-Tametomo-by-Kuniyoshi-Utagawa.png | death_date = {{death date and age|1170|4|23|1139|df=y}} | death_place = [[Higo Province]], Japan | native_name = 源 為朝 | native_name_lang = Japanese | birth_date = 1139 A.D. | parents = [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]] (father) Unknown (mother) | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | battles = [[Hōgen Rebellion]], [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]] }} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Tametomo'''|源 為朝||1139 – April 23, 1170}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''[[Chinzei]] Hachirō Tametomo'''|鎮西 八郎 為朝|}}, was a samurai who fought in the [[Hōgen Rebellion]] of 1156. He was the son of [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]], and brother to [[Minamoto no Yukiie|Yukiie]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo|Yoshitomo]].
Tametomo is known in the epic chronicles as a powerful archer and it is said that he once sunk an entire [[Taira]] ship with a single arrow by puncturing its hull below the waterline. It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]], along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo]], his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of [[Izu Ōshima|Ōshima]] in the [[Izu Islands]]. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing [[seppuku]]. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''[[seppuku]]'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the [[1754 Hōreki River incident]].<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老町の歴史文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away [[Smallpox demon|smallpox demons]], in an 1890 print by [[Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Yoshitoshi]] File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156 (1139–1170)}}{{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Tametomo | image = File:Minamoto-no-Tametomo-by-Kuniyoshi-Utagawa.png | death_date = {{death date and age|1170|4|23|1139|df=y}} | death_place = [[Higo Province]], Japan | native_name = 源 為朝 | native_name_lang = Japanese | birth_date = 1139 A.D. | parents = [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]] (father) Unknown (mother) | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | battles = [[Hōgen Rebellion]], [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]] }} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Tametomo'''|源 為朝||1139 – April 23, 1170}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''[[Chinzei]] Hachirō Tametomo'''|鎮西 八郎 為朝|}}, was a samurai who fought in the [[Hōgen Rebellion]] of 1156. He was the son of [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]], and brother to [[Minamoto no Yukiie|Yukiie]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo|Yoshitomo]].
Tametomo is known in the epic chronicles as a powerful archer and it is said that he once sunk an entire [[Taira]] ship with a single arrow by puncturing its hull below the waterline. It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]], along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo]], his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of [[Izu Ōshima|Ōshima]] in the [[Izu Islands]]. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing [[seppuku]]. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''[[seppuku]]'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the [[1754 Hōreki River incident]].<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老町の歴史文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away [[Smallpox demon|smallpox demons]], in an 1890 print by [[Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Yoshitoshi]] File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>
==See Also== *[[Hokusai]] *[[Takizawa Bakin]] *[[Shunten]] *[[King of Ryukyu]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages and categories from Wikipedia]] to [[Category:kk]][[en:{{subst:FULLPAGENAME}}]][[Category:kk]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Short description|Samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156 (1139–1170)}}{{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Tametomo | image = File:Minamoto-no-Tametomo-by-Kuniyoshi-Utagawa.png | death_date = {{death date and age|1170|4|23|1139|df=y}} | death_place = [[Higo Province]], Japan | native_name = 源 為朝 | native_name_lang = Japanese | birth_date = 1139 A.D. | parents = [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]] (father) Unknown (mother) | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | battles = [[Hōgen Rebellion]], [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]] }} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Tametomo'''|源 為朝||1139 – April 23, 1170}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''[[Chinzei]] Hachirō Tametomo'''|鎮西 八郎 為朝|}}, was a samurai who fought in the [[Hōgen Rebellion]] of 1156. He was the son of [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]], and brother to [[Minamoto no Yukiie|Yukiie]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo|Yoshitomo]].
Tametomo is known in the epic chronicles as a powerful archer and it is said that he once sunk an entire [[Taira]] ship with a single arrow by puncturing its hull below the waterline. It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]], along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo]], his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of [[Izu Ōshima|Ōshima]] in the [[Izu Islands]]. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing [[seppuku]]. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''[[seppuku]]'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the [[1754 Hōreki River incident]].<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老町の歴史文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away [[Smallpox demon|smallpox demons]], in an 1890 print by [[Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Yoshitoshi]] File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>
==See Also== *[[Hokusai]] *[[Takizawa Bakin]] *[[Shunten]] *[[King of Ryukyu]]

He was the son of [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]], and brother to [[Minamoto no Yukiie|Yukiie]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo|Yoshitomo]].

The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.

[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].

revision.text:
{{Short description|Samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156 (1139–1170)}}{{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Tametomo | image = File:Minamoto-no-Tametomo-by-Kuniyoshi-Utagawa.png | death_date = {{death date and age|1170|4|23|1139|df=y}} | death_place = [[Higo Province]], Japan | native_name = 源 為朝 | native_name_lang = Japanese | birth_date = 1139 A.D. | parents = [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]] (father) Unknown (mother) | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | battles = [[Hōgen Rebellion]], [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]] }} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Tametomo'''|源 為朝||1139 – April 23, 1170}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''[[Chinzei]] Hachirō Tametomo'''|鎮西 八郎 為朝|}}, was a samurai who fought in the [[Hōgen Rebellion]] of 1156. He was the son of [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]], and brother to [[Minamoto no Yukiie|Yukiie]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo|Yoshitomo]].
Tametomo is known in the epic chronicles as a powerful archer and it is said that he once sunk an entire [[Taira]] ship with a single arrow by puncturing its hull below the waterline. It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]], along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo]], his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of [[Izu Ōshima|Ōshima]] in the [[Izu Islands]]. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing [[seppuku]]. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''[[seppuku]]'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the [[1754 Hōreki River incident]].<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老町の歴史文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away [[Smallpox demon|smallpox demons]], in an 1890 print by [[Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Yoshitoshi]] File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>
==See Also== *[[Hokusai]] *[[Takizawa Bakin]] *[[Shunten]] *[[King of Ryukyu]]

[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].

[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].

revision.text:
{{Short description|Samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156 (1139–1170)}}{{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Tametomo | image = File:Minamoto-no-Tametomo-by-Kuniyoshi-Utagawa.png | death_date = {{death date and age|1170|4|23|1139|df=y}} | death_place = [[Higo Province]], Japan | native_name = 源 為朝 | native_name_lang = Japanese | birth_date = 1139 A.D. | parents = [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]] (father) Unknown (mother) | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | battles = [[Hōgen Rebellion]], [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]] }} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Tametomo'''|源 為朝||1139 – April 23, 1170}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''[[Chinzei]] Hachirō Tametomo'''|鎮西 八郎 為朝|}}, was a samurai who fought in the [[Hōgen Rebellion]] of 1156. He was the son of [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]], and brother to [[Minamoto no Yukiie|Yukiie]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo|Yoshitomo]].
Tametomo is known in the epic chronicles as a powerful archer and it is said that he once sunk an entire [[Taira]] ship with a single arrow by puncturing its hull below the waterline. It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]], along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo]], his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of [[Izu Ōshima|Ōshima]] in the [[Izu Islands]]. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing [[seppuku]]. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''[[seppuku]]'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the [[1754 Hōreki River incident]].<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老町の歴史文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away [[Smallpox demon|smallpox demons]], in an 1890 print by [[Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Yoshitoshi]] File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>
==See Also== *[[Hokusai]] *[[Takizawa Bakin]] *[[Shunten]] *[[King of Ryukyu]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:kk]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Short description|Samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156 (1139–1170)}}{{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Tametomo | image = File:Minamoto-no-Tametomo-by-Kuniyoshi-Utagawa.png | death_date = {{death date and age|1170|4|23|1139|df=y}} | death_place = [[Higo Province]], Japan | native_name = 源 為朝 | native_name_lang = Japanese | birth_date = 1139 A.D. | parents = [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]] (father) Unknown (mother) | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | battles = [[Hōgen Rebellion]], [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]] }} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Tametomo'''|源 為朝||1139 – April 23, 1170}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''[[Chinzei]] Hachirō Tametomo'''|鎮西 八郎 為朝|}}, was a samurai who fought in the [[Hōgen Rebellion]] of 1156. He was the son of [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]], and brother to [[Minamoto no Yukiie|Yukiie]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo|Yoshitomo]].
Tametomo is known in the epic chronicles as a powerful archer and it is said that he once sunk an entire [[Taira]] ship with a single arrow by puncturing its hull below the waterline. It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]], along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo]], his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of [[Izu Ōshima|Ōshima]] in the [[Izu Islands]]. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing [[seppuku]]. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''[[seppuku]]'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the [[1754 Hōreki River incident]].<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老町の歴史文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away [[Smallpox demon|smallpox demons]], in an 1890 print by [[Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Yoshitoshi]] File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>
==See Also== *[[Hokusai]] *[[Takizawa Bakin]] *[[Shunten]] *[[King of Ryukyu]]

{{Short description|Samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156 (1139–1170)}}{{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Tametomo | image = File:Minamoto-no-Tametomo-by-Kuniyoshi-Utagawa.png | death_date = {{death date and age|1170|4|23|1139|df=y}} | death_place = [[Higo Province]], Japan | native_name = 源 為朝 | native_name_lang = Japanese | birth_date = 1139 A.D. | parents = [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]] (father) Unknown (mother) | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | battles = [[Hōgen Rebellion]], [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]] }} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Tametomo'''|源 為朝||1139 – April 23, 1170}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''[[Chinzei]] Hachirō Tametomo'''|鎮西 八郎 為朝|}}, was a samurai who fought in the [[Hōgen Rebellion]] of 1156. He was the son of [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]], and brother to [[Minamoto no Yukiie|Yukiie]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo|Yoshitomo]].
Tametomo is known in the epic chronicles as a powerful archer and it is said that he once sunk an entire [[Taira]] ship with a single arrow by puncturing its hull below the waterline. It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]], along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo]], his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of [[Izu Ōshima|Ōshima]] in the [[Izu Islands]]. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing [[seppuku]]. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''[[seppuku]]'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the [[1754 Hōreki River incident]].<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老町の歴史文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away [[Smallpox demon|smallpox demons]], in an 1890 print by [[Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Yoshitoshi]] File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>

{{Short description|Samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156 (1139–1170)}}{{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Tametomo | image = File:Minamoto-no-Tametomo-by-Kuniyoshi-Utagawa.png | death_date = {{death date and age|1170|4|23|1139|df=y}} | death_place = [[Higo Province]], Japan | native_name = 源 為朝 | native_name_lang = Japanese | birth_date = 1139 A.D. | parents = [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]] (father) Unknown (mother) | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | battles = [[Hōgen Rebellion]], [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]] }} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Tametomo'''|源 為朝||1139 – April 23, 1170}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''[[Chinzei]] Hachirō Tametomo'''|鎮西 八郎 為朝|}}, was a samurai who fought in the [[Hōgen Rebellion]] of 1156. It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]], along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo]], his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.

Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing [[seppuku]]. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''[[seppuku]]'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>

[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away [[Smallpox demon|smallpox demons]], in an 1890 print by [[Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Yoshitoshi]] File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>

{{Short description|Samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156 (1139–1170)}}{{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Tametomo | image = File:Minamoto-no-Tametomo-by-Kuniyoshi-Utagawa.png | death_date = {{death date and age|1170|4|23|1139|df=y}} | death_place = [[Higo Province]], Japan | native_name = 源 為朝 | native_name_lang = Japanese | birth_date = 1139 A.D. | parents = [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]] (father) Unknown (mother) | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | battles = [[Hōgen Rebellion]], [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]] }} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Tametomo'''|源 為朝||1139 – April 23, 1170}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''[[Chinzei]] Hachirō Tametomo'''|鎮西 八郎 為朝|}}, was a samurai who fought in the [[Hōgen Rebellion]] of 1156. He was the son of [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]], and brother to [[Minamoto no Yukiie|Yukiie]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo|Yoshitomo]].
Tametomo is known in the epic chronicles as a powerful archer and it is said that he once sunk an entire [[Taira]] ship with a single arrow by puncturing its hull below the waterline. It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]], along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo]], his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of [[Izu Ōshima|Ōshima]] in the [[Izu Islands]]. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing [[seppuku]]. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''[[seppuku]]'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the [[1754 Hōreki River incident]].<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老町の歴史文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away [[Smallpox demon|smallpox demons]], in an 1890 print by [[Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Yoshitoshi]] File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>

During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the [[1754 Hōreki River incident]].<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老町の歴史文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].

{{Short description|Samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156 (1139–1170)}}{{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Tametomo | image = File:Minamoto-no-Tametomo-by-Kuniyoshi-Utagawa.png | death_date = {{death date and age|1170|4|23|1139|df=y}} | death_place = [[Higo Province]], Japan | native_name = 源 為朝 | native_name_lang = Japanese | birth_date = 1139 A.D. | parents = [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]] (father) Unknown (mother) | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | battles = [[Hōgen Rebellion]], [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]] }} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Tametomo'''|源 為朝||1139 – April 23, 1170}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''[[Chinzei]] Hachirō Tametomo'''|鎮西 八郎 為朝|}}, was a samurai who fought in the [[Hōgen Rebellion]] of 1156. He was the son of [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]], and brother to [[Minamoto no Yukiie|Yukiie]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo|Yoshitomo]].
Tametomo is known in the epic chronicles as a powerful archer and it is said that he once sunk an entire [[Taira]] ship with a single arrow by puncturing its hull below the waterline. It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]], along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo]], his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of [[Izu Ōshima|Ōshima]] in the [[Izu Islands]]. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing [[seppuku]]. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''[[seppuku]]'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the [[1754 Hōreki River incident]].<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老町の歴史文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away [[Smallpox demon|smallpox demons]], in an 1890 print by [[Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Yoshitoshi]] File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>

Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing [[seppuku]]. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''[[seppuku]]'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the [[1754 Hōreki River incident]].<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老町の歴史文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].

The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of [[Izu Ōshima|Ōshima]] in the [[Izu Islands]]. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing [[seppuku]]. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''[[seppuku]]'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the [[1754 Hōreki River incident]].<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老町の歴史文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away [[Smallpox demon|smallpox demons]], in an 1890 print by [[Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Yoshitoshi]] File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156 (1139–1170)}}{{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Tametomo | image = File:Minamoto-no-Tametomo-by-Kuniyoshi-Utagawa.png | death_date = {{death date and age|1170|4|23|1139|df=y}} | death_place = [[Higo Province]], Japan | native_name = 源 為朝 | native_name_lang = Japanese | birth_date = 1139 A.D. | parents = [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]] (father) Unknown (mother) | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | battles = [[Hōgen Rebellion]], [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]] }} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Tametomo'''|源 為朝||1139 – April 23, 1170}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''[[Chinzei]] Hachirō Tametomo'''|鎮西 八郎 為朝|}}, was a samurai who fought in the [[Hōgen Rebellion]] of 1156. He was the son of [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]], and brother to [[Minamoto no Yukiie|Yukiie]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo|Yoshitomo]].
Tametomo is known in the epic chronicles as a powerful archer and it is said that he once sunk an entire [[Taira]] ship with a single arrow by puncturing its hull below the waterline. It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]], along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo]], his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of [[Izu Ōshima|Ōshima]] in the [[Izu Islands]]. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing [[seppuku]]. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''[[seppuku]]'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the [[1754 Hōreki River incident]].<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老町の歴史文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away [[Smallpox demon|smallpox demons]], in an 1890 print by [[Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Yoshitoshi]] File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>
==See Also== *[[Hokusai]] *[[Takizawa Bakin]] *[[Shunten]] *[[King of Ryukyu]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "qqq" to "qq"
revision.text:
{{Short description|Samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156 (1139–1170)}}{{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Tametomo | image = File:Minamoto-no-Tametomo-by-Kuniyoshi-Utagawa.png | death_date = {{death date and age|1170|4|23|1139|df=y}} | death_place = [[Higo Province]], Japan | native_name = 源 為朝 | native_name_lang = Japanese | birth_date = 1139 A.D. | parents = [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]] (father) Unknown (mother) | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | battles = [[Hōgen Rebellion]], [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]] }} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Tametomo'''|源 為朝||1139 – April 23, 1170}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''[[Chinzei]] Hachirō Tametomo'''|鎮西 八郎 為朝|}}, was a samurai who fought in the [[Hōgen Rebellion]] of 1156. He was the son of [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]], and brother to [[Minamoto no Yukiie|Yukiie]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo|Yoshitomo]].
Tametomo is known in the epic chronicles as a powerful archer and it is said that he once sunk an entire [[Taira]] ship with a single arrow by puncturing its hull below the waterline. It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]], along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo]], his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of [[Izu Ōshima|Ōshima]] in the [[Izu Islands]]. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing [[seppuku]]. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''[[seppuku]]'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the [[1754 Hōreki River incident]].<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老町の歴史文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away [[Smallpox demon|smallpox demons]], in an 1890 print by [[Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Yoshitoshi]] File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>
==See Also== *[[Hokusai]] *[[Takizawa Bakin]] *[[Shunten]] *[[King of Ryukyu]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156 (1139–1170)}}{{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Tametomo | image = File:Minamoto-no-Tametomo-by-Kuniyoshi-Utagawa.png | death_date = {{death date and age|1170|4|23|1139|df=y}} | death_place = [[Higo Province]], Japan | native_name = 源 為朝 | native_name_lang = Japanese | birth_date = 1139 A.D. | parents = [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]] (father) Unknown (mother) | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | battles = [[Hōgen Rebellion]], [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]] }} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Tametomo'''|源 為朝||1139 – April 23, 1170}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''[[Chinzei]] Hachirō Tametomo'''|鎮西 八郎 為朝|}}, was a samurai who fought in the [[Hōgen Rebellion]] of 1156. He was the son of [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]], and brother to [[Minamoto no Yukiie|Yukiie]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo|Yoshitomo]].
Tametomo is known in the epic chronicles as a powerful archer and it is said that he once sunk an entire [[Taira]] ship with a single arrow by puncturing its hull below the waterline. It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]], along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo]], his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of [[Izu Ōshima|Ōshima]] in the [[Izu Islands]]. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing [[seppuku]]. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''[[seppuku]]'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the [[1754 Hōreki River incident]].<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老町の歴史文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away [[Smallpox demon|smallpox demons]], in an 1890 print by [[Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Yoshitoshi]] File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>
==See Also== *[[Hokusai]] *[[Takizawa Bakin]] *[[Shunten]] *[[King of Ryukyu]]

It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]], along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo]], his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.

revision.text:
{{Short description|Samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156 (1139–1170)}}{{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Tametomo | image = File:Minamoto-no-Tametomo-by-Kuniyoshi-Utagawa.png | death_date = {{death date and age|1170|4|23|1139|df=y}} | death_place = [[Higo Province]], Japan | native_name = 源 為朝 | native_name_lang = Japanese | birth_date = 1139 A.D. | parents = [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]] (father) Unknown (mother) | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | battles = [[Hōgen Rebellion]], [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]] }} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Tametomo'''|源 為朝||1139 – April 23, 1170}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''[[Chinzei]] Hachirō Tametomo'''|鎮西 八郎 為朝|}}, was a samurai who fought in the [[Hōgen Rebellion]] of 1156. He was the son of [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]], and brother to [[Minamoto no Yukiie|Yukiie]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo|Yoshitomo]].
Tametomo is known in the epic chronicles as a powerful archer and it is said that he once sunk an entire [[Taira]] ship with a single arrow by puncturing its hull below the waterline. It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]], along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo]], his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of [[Izu Ōshima|Ōshima]] in the [[Izu Islands]]. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing [[seppuku]]. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''[[seppuku]]'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the [[1754 Hōreki River incident]].<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老町の歴史文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away [[Smallpox demon|smallpox demons]], in an 1890 print by [[Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Yoshitoshi]] File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>
==See Also== *[[Hokusai]] *[[Takizawa Bakin]] *[[Shunten]] *[[King of Ryukyu]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Short description|Samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156 (1139–1170)}}{{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Tametomo | image = File:Minamoto-no-Tametomo-by-Kuniyoshi-Utagawa.png | death_date = {{death date and age|1170|4|23|1139|df=y}} | death_place = [[Higo Province]], Japan | native_name = 源 為朝 | native_name_lang = Japanese | birth_date = 1139 A.D. | parents = [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]] (father) Unknown (mother) | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | battles = [[Hōgen Rebellion]], [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]] }} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Tametomo'''|源 為朝||1139 – April 23, 1170}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''[[Chinzei]] Hachirō Tametomo'''|鎮西 八郎 為朝|}}, was a samurai who fought in the [[Hōgen Rebellion]] of 1156. He was the son of [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]], and brother to [[Minamoto no Yukiie|Yukiie]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo|Yoshitomo]].
Tametomo is known in the epic chronicles as a powerful archer and it is said that he once sunk an entire [[Taira]] ship with a single arrow by puncturing its hull below the waterline. It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]], along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo]], his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of [[Izu Ōshima|Ōshima]] in the [[Izu Islands]]. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing [[seppuku]]. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''[[seppuku]]'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the [[1754 Hōreki River incident]].<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老町の歴史文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away [[Smallpox demon|smallpox demons]], in an 1890 print by [[Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Yoshitoshi]] File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>
==See Also== *[[Hokusai]] *[[Takizawa Bakin]] *[[Shunten]] *[[King of Ryukyu]]

<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away [[Smallpox demon|smallpox demons]], in an 1890 print by [[Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Yoshitoshi]] File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>

{{Short description|Samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156 (1139–1170)}}{{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Tametomo | image = File:Minamoto-no-Tametomo-by-Kuniyoshi-Utagawa.png | death_date = {{death date and age|1170|4|23|1139|df=y}} | death_place = [[Higo Province]], Japan | native_name = 源 為朝 | native_name_lang = Japanese | birth_date = 1139 A.D. | parents = [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]] (father) Unknown (mother) | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | battles = [[Hōgen Rebellion]], [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]] }} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Tametomo'''|源 為朝||1139 – April 23, 1170}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''[[Chinzei]] Hachirō Tametomo'''|鎮西 八郎 為朝|}}, was a samurai who fought in the [[Hōgen Rebellion]] of 1156. He was the son of [[Minamoto no Tameyoshi]], and brother to [[Minamoto no Yukiie|Yukiie]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo|Yoshitomo]].
Tametomo is known in the epic chronicles as a powerful archer and it is said that he once sunk an entire [[Taira]] ship with a single arrow by puncturing its hull below the waterline. It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the [[Siege of Shirakawa-den]], along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and [[Minamoto no Yoshitomo]], his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of [[Izu Ōshima|Ōshima]] in the [[Izu Islands]]. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing [[seppuku]]. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''[[seppuku]]'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the [[1754 Hōreki River incident]].<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老町の歴史文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on [[Takizawa Bakin]]’s novel ''{{Interlanguage link multi|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist [[Hokusai]].
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away [[Smallpox demon|smallpox demons]], in an 1890 print by [[Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Yoshitoshi]] File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>
