{{ill|samurai|WD=Q38142}}|children={{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsugu|WD=Q109362145}}; {{ill|Q106615942|WD=Q106615942}}; {{ill|Shunten|WD=Q1059918}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Tameyoshi|WD=Q1381638}}|mother={{ill|Q110407703|WD=Q110407703}}|family={{ill|Kawachi Genji|WD=Q5360652}}; {{ill|Seiwa Genji|WD=Q2164991}}|native_name=源為朝|given_name={{ill|Tametomo|WD=Q125466176}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1339356]] Wikidata name: Minamoto no Tametomo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156 (1139–1170)}}{{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Tametomo | image = File:Minamoto-no-Tametomo-by-Kuniyoshi-Utagawa.png | death_date = {{death date and age|1170|4|23|1139|df=y}} | death_place = {{ill|Higo Province|en|Higo Province|WD=Q259705|lt=Higo Province}}, Japan | native_name = 源 為朝 | native_name_lang = Japanese | birth_date = 1139 A.D. | parents = {{ill|Minamoto no Tameyoshi|en|Minamoto no Tameyoshi|WD=Q1381638|lt=Minamoto no Tameyoshi}} (father) Unknown (mother) | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | battles = [[:en:Hōgen Rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]], {{ill|Siege of Shirakawa-den|en|Siege of Shirakawa-den|WD=Q3486062|lt=Siege of Shirakawa-den}} }} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Tametomo'''|源 為朝||1139 – April 23, 1170}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Chinzei|en|Chinzei|WD=Q2963795|lt=Chinzei}} Hachirō Tametomo'''|鎮西 八郎 為朝|}}, was a samurai who fought in the [[:en:Hōgen Rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]] of 1156. He was the son of {{ill|Minamoto no Tameyoshi|en|Minamoto no Tameyoshi|WD=Q1381638|lt=Minamoto no Tameyoshi}}, and brother to {{ill|Minamoto no Yukiie|en|Minamoto no Yukiie|WD=Q2376525|lt=Yukiie}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitomo|en|Minamoto no Yoshitomo|WD=Q532301|lt=Yoshitomo}}.
Tametomo is known in the epic chronicles as a powerful archer and it is said that he once sunk an entire [[Taira clan|Taira]] ship with a single arrow by puncturing its hull below the waterline. It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the {{ill|Siege of Shirakawa-den|en|Siege of Shirakawa-den|WD=Q3486062|lt=Siege of Shirakawa-den}}, along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitomo|en|Minamoto no Yoshitomo|WD=Q532301|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitomo}}, his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[:en:Hōgen Rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of {{ill|Izu Ōshima|en|Izu Ōshima|WD=Q1070580|lt=Ōshima}} in the {{ill|Izu Islands|en|Izu Islands|WD=Q864302|lt=Izu Islands}}. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing {{ill|Seppuku|en|Seppuku|WD=Q133462|lt=Seppuku}}. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''{{ill|Seppuku|en|Seppuku|WD=Q133462|lt=Seppuku}}'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the {{ill|1754 Hōreki River incident#Aburajima Embankment National Historic Site|en|1754 Hōreki River incident#Aburajima Embankment National Historic Site|WD=Q4552908|lt=1754 Hōreki River incident#Aburajima Embankment National Historic Site}}.<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老の Municipal History文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[:en:Hokusai Katsushika|Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on {{ill|Takizawa Bakin|en|Takizawa Bakin|WD=Q463142|lt=Takizawa Bakin}}’s novel ''{{ill|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|qq=}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist {{ill|Hokusai|en|Hokusai|WD=Q5586|lt=Hokusai}}.
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away {{ill|Smallpox demon|en|Smallpox demon|WD=Q7543689|lt=smallpox demons}}, in an 1890 print by {{ill|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|en|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|WD=Q467337|lt=Yoshitoshi}} File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>
==See Also== *{{ill|Hokusai|en|Hokusai|WD=Q5586|lt=Hokusai}} *{{ill|Takizawa Bakin|en|Takizawa Bakin|WD=Q463142|lt=Takizawa Bakin}} *{{ill|Shunten|en|Shunten|WD=Q1059918|lt=Shunten}} *{{ill|King of Ryukyu|en|King of Ryukyu|WD=Q1336751|lt=King of Ryukyu}}
==References== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category|Minamoto no Tametomo}} {{reflist|30em}}

Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing {{ill|Seppuku|en|Seppuku|WD=Q133462|lt=Seppuku}}. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''{{ill|Seppuku|en|Seppuku|WD=Q133462|lt=Seppuku}}'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the {{ill|1754 Hōreki River incident#Aburajima Embankment National Historic Site|en|1754 Hōreki River incident#Aburajima Embankment National Historic Site|WD=Q4552908|lt=1754 Hōreki River incident#Aburajima Embankment National Historic Site}}.<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老の Municipal History文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[:en:Hokusai Katsushika|Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on {{ill|Takizawa Bakin|en|Takizawa Bakin|WD=Q463142|lt=Takizawa Bakin}}’s novel ''{{ill|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|qq=}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist {{ill|Hokusai|en|Hokusai|WD=Q5586|lt=Hokusai}}.
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away {{ill|Smallpox demon|en|Smallpox demon|WD=Q7543689|lt=smallpox demons}}, in an 1890 print by {{ill|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|en|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|WD=Q467337|lt=Yoshitoshi}} File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Minamoto no Tametomo]][[en:Minamoto no Tametomo]][[es:Minamoto no Tametomo]][[fa:میناموتو نو تامه‌تومو]][[fr:Minamoto no Tametomo]][[it:Minamoto no Tametomo]][[ja:源為朝]][[ko:미나모토노 다메토모]][[pt:Minamoto no Tametomo]][[ru:Минамото-но Тамэтомо]][[sv:Minamoto no Tametomo]][[uk:Мінамото-но Таметомо]][[zh:源為朝]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Minamoto no Tametomo]][[en:Minamoto no Tametomo]][[es:Minamoto no Tametomo]][[fa:میناموتو نو تامه‌تومو]][[fr:Minamoto no Tametomo]][[it:Minamoto no Tametomo]][[ja:源為朝]][[ko:미나모토노 다메토모]][[pt:Minamoto no Tametomo]][[ru:Минамото-но Тамэтомо]][[sv:Minamoto no Tametomo]][[uk:Мінамото-но Таметомо]][[zh:源為朝]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Minamoto no Tametomo]][[en:Minamoto no Tametomo]][[es:Minamoto no Tametomo]][[fa:میناموتو نو تامه‌تومو]][[fr:Minamoto no Tametomo]][[it:Minamoto no Tametomo]][[ja:源為朝]][[ko:미나모토노 다메토모]][[pt:Minamoto no Tametomo]][[ru:Минамото-но Тамэтомо]][[sv:Minamoto no Tametomo]][[uk:Мінамото-но Таметомо]][[zh:源為朝]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Minamoto-no-Tametomo-by-Kuniyoshi-Utagawa.png|birth_date=1139|death_date={{death date|1170|4|23}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|military personnel|WD=Q47064}}; {{ill|samurai|WD=Q38142}}|children={{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsugu|WD=Q109362145}}; {{ill|Q106615942|WD=Q106615942}}; {{ill|Shunten|WD=Q1059918}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Tameyoshi|WD=Q1381638}}|mother={{ill|Q110407703|WD=Q110407703}}|family={{ill|Kawachi Genji|WD=Q5360652}}; {{ill|Seiwa Genji|WD=Q2164991}}|native_name=源為朝|given_name={{ill|Tametomo|WD=Q125466176}}}} {{Short description|Samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156 (1139–1170)}}{{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Tametomo | image = File:Minamoto-no-Tametomo-by-Kuniyoshi-Utagawa.png | death_date = {{death date and age|1170|4|23|1139|df=y}} | death_place = {{ill|Higo Province|en|Higo Province|WD=Q259705|lt=Higo Province}}, Japan | native_name = 源 為朝 | native_name_lang = Japanese | birth_date = 1139 A.D. | parents = {{ill|Minamoto no Tameyoshi|en|Minamoto no Tameyoshi|WD=Q1381638|lt=Minamoto no Tameyoshi}} (father) Unknown (mother) | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | battles = [[:en:Hōgen Rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]], {{ill|Siege of Shirakawa-den|en|Siege of Shirakawa-den|WD=Q3486062|lt=Siege of Shirakawa-den}} }} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Tametomo'''|源 為朝||1139 – April 23, 1170}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Chinzei|en|Chinzei|WD=Q2963795|lt=Chinzei}} Hachirō Tametomo'''|鎮西 八郎 為朝|}}, was a samurai who fought in the [[:en:Hōgen Rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]] of 1156. He was the son of {{ill|Minamoto no Tameyoshi|en|Minamoto no Tameyoshi|WD=Q1381638|lt=Minamoto no Tameyoshi}}, and brother to {{ill|Minamoto no Yukiie|en|Minamoto no Yukiie|WD=Q2376525|lt=Yukiie}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitomo|en|Minamoto no Yoshitomo|WD=Q532301|lt=Yoshitomo}}.
Tametomo is known in the epic chronicles as a powerful archer and it is said that he once sunk an entire [[Taira clan|Taira]] ship with a single arrow by puncturing its hull below the waterline. It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the {{ill|Siege of Shirakawa-den|en|Siege of Shirakawa-den|WD=Q3486062|lt=Siege of Shirakawa-den}}, along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitomo|en|Minamoto no Yoshitomo|WD=Q532301|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitomo}}, his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[:en:Hōgen Rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of {{ill|Izu Ōshima|en|Izu Ōshima|WD=Q1070580|lt=Ōshima}} in the {{ill|Izu Islands|en|Izu Islands|WD=Q864302|lt=Izu Islands}}. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing {{ill|Seppuku|en|Seppuku|WD=Q133462|lt=Seppuku}}. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''{{ill|Seppuku|en|Seppuku|WD=Q133462|lt=Seppuku}}'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the {{ill|1754 Hōreki River incident#Aburajima Embankment National Historic Site|en|1754 Hōreki River incident#Aburajima Embankment National Historic Site|WD=Q4552908|lt=1754 Hōreki River incident#Aburajima Embankment National Historic Site}}.<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老の Municipal History文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[:en:Hokusai Katsushika|Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on {{ill|Takizawa Bakin|en|Takizawa Bakin|WD=Q463142|lt=Takizawa Bakin}}’s novel ''{{ill|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|qq=}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist {{ill|Hokusai|en|Hokusai|WD=Q5586|lt=Hokusai}}.
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away {{ill|Smallpox demon|en|Smallpox demon|WD=Q7543689|lt=smallpox demons}}, in an 1890 print by {{ill|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|en|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|WD=Q467337|lt=Yoshitoshi}} File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>

{{ill|samurai|WD=Q38142|1=Samurai}}|children={{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsugu|WD=Q109362145|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsugu}}; {{ill|Q106615942|WD=Q106615942|1=Q106615942}}; {{ill|Shunten|WD=Q1059918|1=Shunten}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Tameyoshi|WD=Q1381638|1=Minamoto no Tameyoshi}}|mother={{ill|Q110407703|WD=Q110407703|1=Q110407703}}|family={{ill|Kawachi Genji|WD=Q5360652|1=Kawachi Genji}}; {{ill|Seiwa Genji|WD=Q2164991|1=Seiwa Genji}}|native_name=源為朝|given_name={{ill|Tametomo|WD=Q125466176|1=Tametomo}}}} {{Short description|Samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156 (1139–1170)}}{{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Tametomo | image = File:Minamoto-no-Tametomo-by-Kuniyoshi-Utagawa.png | death_date = {{death date and age|1170|4|23|1139|df=y}} | death_place = {{ill|Higo Province|en|Higo Province|WD=Q259705|lt=Higo Province|1=Higo Province}}, Japan | native_name = 源 為朝 | native_name_lang = Japanese | birth_date = 1139 A.D. | parents = {{ill|Minamoto no Tameyoshi|en|Minamoto no Tameyoshi|WD=Q1381638|lt=Minamoto no Tameyoshi|1=Minamoto no Tameyoshi}} (father) Unknown (mother) | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | battles = [[:en:Hōgen Rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]], {{ill|Siege of Shirakawa-den|en|Siege of Shirakawa-den|WD=Q3486062|lt=Siege of Shirakawa-den|1=Siege of Shirakawa-den}} }} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Tametomo'''|源 為朝||1139 – April 23, 1170}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Chinzei|en|Chinzei|WD=Q2963795|lt=Chinzei|1=Chinzei}} Hachirō Tametomo'''|鎮西 八郎 為朝|}}, was a samurai who fought in the [[:en:Hōgen Rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]] of 1156. He was the son of {{ill|Minamoto no Tameyoshi|en|Minamoto no Tameyoshi|WD=Q1381638|lt=Minamoto no Tameyoshi|1=Minamoto no Tameyoshi}}, and brother to {{ill|Minamoto no Yukiie|en|Minamoto no Yukiie|WD=Q2376525|lt=Yukiie|1=Minamoto no Yukiie}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitomo|en|Minamoto no Yoshitomo|WD=Q532301|lt=Yoshitomo|1=Minamoto no Yoshitomo}}.
Tametomo is known in the epic chronicles as a powerful archer and it is said that he once sunk an entire [[Taira clan|Taira]] ship with a single arrow by puncturing its hull below the waterline. It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the {{ill|Siege of Shirakawa-den|en|Siege of Shirakawa-den|WD=Q3486062|lt=Siege of Shirakawa-den|1=Siege of Shirakawa-den}}, along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitomo|en|Minamoto no Yoshitomo|WD=Q532301|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitomo|1=Minamoto no Yoshitomo}}, his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[:en:Hōgen Rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of {{ill|Izu Ōshima|en|Izu Ōshima|WD=Q1070580|lt=Ōshima|1=Izu Ōshima}} in the {{ill|Izu Islands|en|Izu Islands|WD=Q864302|lt=Izu Islands|1=Izu Islands}}. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing {{ill|Seppuku|en|Seppuku|WD=Q133462|lt=Seppuku|1=Seppuku}}. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''{{ill|Seppuku|en|Seppuku|WD=Q133462|lt=Seppuku|1=Seppuku}}'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the {{ill|1754 Hōreki River incident#Aburajima Embankment National Historic Site|en|1754 Hōreki River incident#Aburajima Embankment National Historic Site|WD=Q4552908|lt=1754 Hōreki River incident#Aburajima Embankment National Historic Site|1=1754 Hōreki River incident}}.<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老の Municipal History文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[:en:Hokusai Katsushika|Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on {{ill|Takizawa Bakin|en|Takizawa Bakin|WD=Q463142|lt=Takizawa Bakin|1=Takizawa Bakin}}’s novel ''{{ill|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|qq=}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist {{ill|Hokusai|en|Hokusai|WD=Q5586|lt=Hokusai|1=Hokusai}}.
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away {{ill|Smallpox demon|en|Smallpox demon|WD=Q7543689|lt=smallpox demons|1=Smallpox demon}}, in an 1890 print by {{ill|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|en|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|WD=Q467337|lt=Yoshitoshi|1=Tsukioka Yoshitoshi}} File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>
==See Also== *{{ill|Hokusai|en|Hokusai|WD=Q5586|lt=Hokusai|1=Hokusai}} *{{ill|Takizawa Bakin|en|Takizawa Bakin|WD=Q463142|lt=Takizawa Bakin|1=Takizawa Bakin}} *{{ill|Shunten|en|Shunten|WD=Q1059918|lt=Shunten|1=Shunten}} *{{ill|King of Ryukyu|en|King of Ryukyu|WD=Q1336751|lt=King of Ryukyu|1=King of Ryukyu}}

After the [[:en:Hōgen Rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of {{ill|Izu Ōshima|en|Izu Ōshima|WD=Q1070580|lt=Ōshima|1=Izu Ōshima}} in the {{ill|Izu Islands|en|Izu Islands|WD=Q864302|lt=Izu Islands|1=Izu Islands}}. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing {{ill|Seppuku|en|Seppuku|WD=Q133462|lt=Seppuku|1=Seppuku}}. [[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[:en:Hokusai Katsushika|Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on {{ill|Takizawa Bakin|en|Takizawa Bakin|WD=Q463142|lt=Takizawa Bakin|1=Takizawa Bakin}}’s novel ''{{ill|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|qq=}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist {{ill|Hokusai|en|Hokusai|WD=Q5586|lt=Hokusai|1=Hokusai}}.

{{ill|samurai|qid=Q38142|1=Samurai}}|children={{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsugu|qid=Q109362145|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsugu}}; {{ill|Q106615942|qid=Q106615942|1=Q106615942}}; {{ill|Shunten|qid=Q1059918|1=Shunten}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Tameyoshi|qid=Q1381638|1=Minamoto no Tameyoshi}}|mother={{ill|Q110407703|qid=Q110407703|1=Q110407703}}|family={{ill|Kawachi Genji|qid=Q5360652|1=Kawachi Genji}}; {{ill|Seiwa Genji|qid=Q2164991|1=Seiwa Genji}}|native_name=源為朝|given_name={{ill|Tametomo|qid=Q125466176|1=Tametomo}}}} {{Short description|Samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156 (1139–1170)}}{{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Tametomo | image = File:Minamoto-no-Tametomo-by-Kuniyoshi-Utagawa.png | death_date = {{death date and age|1170|4|23|1139|df=y}} | death_place = {{ill|Higo Province|en|Higo Province|qid=Q259705|lt=Higo Province|1=Higo Province}}, Japan | native_name = 源 為朝 | native_name_lang = Japanese | birth_date = 1139 A.D. | parents = {{ill|Minamoto no Tameyoshi|en|Minamoto no Tameyoshi|qid=Q1381638|lt=Minamoto no Tameyoshi|1=Minamoto no Tameyoshi}} (father) Unknown (mother) | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | battles = [[:en:Hōgen Rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]], {{ill|Siege of Shirakawa-den|en|Siege of Shirakawa-den|qid=Q3486062|lt=Siege of Shirakawa-den|1=Siege of Shirakawa-den}} }} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Tametomo'''|源 為朝||1139 – April 23, 1170}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Chinzei|en|Chinzei|qid=Q2963795|lt=Chinzei|1=Chinzei}} Hachirō Tametomo'''|鎮西 八郎 為朝|}}, was a samurai who fought in the [[:en:Hōgen Rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]] of 1156. He was the son of {{ill|Minamoto no Tameyoshi|en|Minamoto no Tameyoshi|qid=Q1381638|lt=Minamoto no Tameyoshi|1=Minamoto no Tameyoshi}}, and brother to {{ill|Minamoto no Yukiie|en|Minamoto no Yukiie|qid=Q2376525|lt=Yukiie|1=Minamoto no Yukiie}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitomo|en|Minamoto no Yoshitomo|qid=Q532301|lt=Yoshitomo|1=Minamoto no Yoshitomo}}.
Tametomo is known in the epic chronicles as a powerful archer and it is said that he once sunk an entire [[Taira clan|Taira]] ship with a single arrow by puncturing its hull below the waterline. It is also added in many legends that his left arm was about 4 inches longer than his right, enabling a longer draw of the arrow, and more powerful shots.<ref name=Turnbull3>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai, A Military History |publisher=MacMillan Publishing Co., Inc. |year=1977 |ISBN=0026205408 |page=35,37}}</ref> He fought in the {{ill|Siege of Shirakawa-den|en|Siege of Shirakawa-den|qid=Q3486062|lt=Siege of Shirakawa-den|1=Siege of Shirakawa-den}}, along with his father, against the forces of [[Taira no Kiyomori]] and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitomo|en|Minamoto no Yoshitomo|qid=Q532301|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitomo|1=Minamoto no Yoshitomo}}, his brother. The palace was set aflame,<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> and Tametomo was forced to flee.
After the [[:en:Hōgen Rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]], the Taira cut the sinews of Tametomo's left arm, limiting the use of his bow, and then he was banished to the island of {{ill|Izu Ōshima|en|Izu Ōshima|qid=Q1070580|lt=Ōshima|1=Izu Ōshima}} in the {{ill|Izu Islands|en|Izu Islands|qid=Q864302|lt=Izu Islands|1=Izu Islands}}. Tametomo eventually killed himself by slicing his abdomen, or committing {{ill|Seppuku|en|Seppuku|qid=Q133462|lt=Seppuku|1=Seppuku}}. He is quite possibly the first warrior to commit ''{{ill|Seppuku|en|Seppuku|qid=Q133462|lt=Seppuku|1=Seppuku}}'' in the chronicles.<ref name=Turnbull3/>
During the Edo period, a descendant of Tametomo named {{Nihongo|[[Kitō Heinai]]|鬼頭 兵内}} was involved in the {{ill|1754 Hōreki River incident#Aburajima Embankment National Historic Site|en|1754 Hōreki River incident#Aburajima Embankment National Historic Site|qid=Q4552908|lt=1754 Hōreki River incident#Aburajima Embankment National Historic Site|1=1754 Hōreki River incident}}.<ref>{{cite web |title=鬼頭兵内氏神 Ujigami of Kitō Heinai |url=https://geoalpha4.sakura.ne.jp/Tagizou_web/point/detail/805/ |website=岐阜県養老の Municipal History文化資源 Gifuken Yōrōchō no Rekishi Bunka Shigen |access-date=17 July 2022}}</ref>
[[File:Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki.jpg|thumb|Chinsetsu Yumihari-zuki, by [[:en:Hokusai Katsushika|Hokusai Katsushika]]]] He is the focus on {{ill|Takizawa Bakin|en|Takizawa Bakin|qid=Q463142|lt=Takizawa Bakin|1=Takizawa Bakin}}’s novel ''{{ill|Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|ja|3=椿説弓張月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinsetsu yumiharizuki|qq=}}'' (Strange Tales of the Crescent Moon). Which was illustrated by famous artist {{ill|Hokusai|en|Hokusai|qid=Q5586|lt=Hokusai|1=Hokusai}}.
<gallery mode="packed" style="text-align: center;' caption=" gallery"="" widths="200" heights="220"> File:Yoshitoshi Driving away the Demons.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo chasing away {{ill|Smallpox demon|en|Smallpox demon|qid=Q7543689|lt=smallpox demons|1=Smallpox demon}}, in an 1890 print by {{ill|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|en|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|qid=Q467337|lt=Yoshitoshi|1=Tsukioka Yoshitoshi}} File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo and Oniyashi on a Beach LACMA M.84.31.244.jpg|Tametomo and Oniyashi on a beach File:Minamoto no Tametomo with a Bow LACMA M.84.31.270.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with a bow File:Chinzei Hachiro Tametomo with Two Islanders on the Beach at Ashijima LACMA M.84.31.92.jpg|Minamoto no Tametomo with two islanders on a beach at Ashijima </gallery>
